summary: The cookie path and domain handed to the pycookiecheat helper must reach Python as argv, never interpolated into the -c script source; a regen that rebuilds the script by fmt.Sprintf reintroduces a code-injection / broken-literal path.
reason: cookiePath is filepath.Join(os.UserHomeDir(), <Chrome profile dir>, "Cookies"). Home directories and profile names can legally contain double quotes or backslashes on macOS/Linux, which would break the Python string literal or inject arbitrary code executed under the user's own interpreter. The static script reads sys.argv[1] (domain) and sys.argv[2] (cookie file, empty -> None) so no untrusted value is ever spliced into source.
validated_outcome: go build/vet/test pass; the pycookiecheat script is a constant with the domain and path passed only as process arguments.
